skill.name: using-beads-bv
skill.description: Coordinate dependency-aware task planning with the beads bd CLI and bv graph sidecar for ready work, priorities, and robot-mode outputs.
skill.summary: Plan and track dependent engineering work with Beads, bv graph analysis, and deterministic CLI workflows.
skill.author: AgentWorkforce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-command patterns and several weak-crypto patterns. The weak-crypto detections are false positives, and the network finding is only a project link, but the skill does instruct agents to run bd, bv, and git commands that can alter local and remote state. No prompt injection, credential access, obfuscation, or data exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Can Modify Project State
medium_findings.description: The skill provides command workflows for bd and bv, including creating, updating, closing, and syncing tasks. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can change project task data and should run only in trusted repositories with the expected tools installed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and semantically aligned with the skill. They are not malicious, but they do create and mutate task records.

medium_findings.title: Session Close Protocol Includes Git Push
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to stage files, commit changes, sync Beads state, and push to a remote repository before ending a session. This can publish local code and task metadata if followed without human review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The git workflow is directly shown in the session close protocol. The risk is clear because git push sends repository state to a remote.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Project URL Is Informational
low_findings.description: The static network finding points to the upstream Beads project URL. It is a documentation link and does not perform a network request or send data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 12 is a Markdown link to the referenced project. No executable network call is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-crypto detections occur on metadata, prose, or command-table lines. No hash function, encryption function, or cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found task-planning prose and command labels at those lines, not cryptographic code. The file is Markdown documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains multiple shell command blocks and inline commands for bd, bv, and git. These are expected for a CLI workflow skill, but they require normal command-execution controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell command blocks are explicit and numerous. Their purpose is legitimate, but command execution remains the relevant dangerous pattern.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when dependent work is tracked across scattered notes and stale task lists. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Beads and bv commands for ready work, graph planning, and session closeout.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Beads instead of a simple session task list.
Lists bd commands for ready work, task details, updates, creation, and closure.
Shows bv robot-mode commands for graph insights, plans, priorities, recipes, and diffs.
Defines priority levels from P0 through P4 with intended usage.
Documents conventions for issue identifiers in commits and messages.
Provides a session close protocol for syncing tasks and repository changes.
limitations: Requires bd and bv to be installed and configured outside the skill.
Does not validate command output or enforce repository permissions.
Does not provide a full Beads tutorial or troubleshooting guide.
Includes git push guidance that should be reviewed before use.

use_cases.title: Select Ready Engineering Work
use_cases.description: Find open work with no blockers, inspect dependencies, and claim the next issue in a Beads queue.

use_cases.title: Plan Parallel Agent Execution
use_cases.description: Use bv robot outputs to understand graph metrics, parallel tracks, and items that unblock other work.

use_cases.title: Close a Coordinated Session
use_cases.description: Sync Beads state, include task identifiers in commits, and prepare local work for remote publication.

prompt_templates.title: Find Ready Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Beads workflow to find ready work, show the most relevant issue, and explain why it is unblocked.

prompt_templates.title: Claim a Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the selected Beads issue, summarize its dependencies, and update it to in progress if it is ready.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Parallel Tracks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bv robot planning to identify parallel tracks, priority recommendations, and the work that unblocks the most issues.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Session Closeout
prompt_templates.prompt: Follow the Beads close protocol, list pending sync or git actions, and highlight anything that needs review before pushing.

output_examples.input: Find the next issue that is ready to work.
output_examples.output: The response identifies unblocked Beads issues, recommends one issue, and explains the dependency reason for that choice.

output_examples.input: Plan work for three agents using bv.
output: A graph-based execution plan with parallel work tracks.
Priority notes based on blockers and unblocking value.
A short list of items that require human coordination.

output_examples.output: The response checks task status, sync requirements, commit identifiers, and remote push readiness before ending the session.

best_practices: Run bv with robot flags so agents receive deterministic, noninteractive output.
Use Beads issue identifiers in commit messages and coordination notes.
Review git staging, commits, and pushes before publishing repository changes.
anti_patterns: Using bv without robot flags in an automated assistant session.
Tracking coordinated work only in Markdown while Beads is the source of truth.
Using high, medium, or low labels instead of P0 through P4 priorities.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It gives assistants a consistent workflow for dependency-aware task planning with Beads and bv.

faq.question: Does this skill install bd or bv?
faq.answer: No. The tools must already be installed and configured in the project environment.

faq.question: Why are robot flags important?
faq.answer: Robot flags avoid interactive bv screens and return structured output suitable for AI agents.

faq.question: Can this skill change my repository?
faq.answer: Yes, if the suggested bd or git commands are executed. Review commands before running them.

faq.question: Is this only for multi-agent projects?
faq.answer: No. It can help single contributors, but it is most useful when dependencies and parallel work matter.

faq.question: Should I use this for simple one-session tasks?
faq.answer: No. The skill itself recommends using a simple task list for small single-session work.
